content:
- User asked to make all three docs story tasks high priority.
- Searched local Ralph conventions and found task selection expects `<priority>` tags such as `<priority>high</priority>`.
- Added `<priority>high</priority>` directly under the title line in all three story-docs-k2-expansion task files and verified the insertion.

- Read progress log, .ralph/current_tasks.md, and .ralph/current_tasks_done.md.
- Checked all active candidate task files directly for <priority> and <blocked_by>; no blocker tags were present and done list was empty.
- No unblocked failing bug tasks existed. Highest-priority failing tasks were the docs high-priority tasks. Chose the first docs-story task because it generates the chapter content that tasks 02 and 03 explicitly depend on for linking and final verification.
- Wrote .ralph/tasks/story-docs-k2-expansion/01-task-run-k2-docs-loop-in-five-way-parallel-batches.md to .ralph/current_task.txt.

- Read Ralph progress, current task, and saw this task was already in NOW EXECUTE state, so I executed rather than replanning.
- Ran five parallel k2-docs-loop choose-doc lanes. Needed rerolls because one lane returned a malformed tool-call artifact and another duplicated an existing target path; final unique targets were docs/src/reference/cli-commands.md, docs/src/reference/pgtuskmasterctl-cli.md, docs/src/reference/cli.md, docs/src/reference/runtime-configuration.md, and docs/src/reference/cli-pgtuskmasterctl.md.
- Gathered requested raw files plus wrote verbose extra context files at docs/tmp/verbose_extra_context/cli-surface-summary.md and docs/tmp/verbose_extra_context/runtime-config-summary.md.
- Ran five parallel prepare-draft lanes successfully. Drafts landed under docs/draft/docs/src/reference/.
- Did factual review. Marked unsupported claims in runtime-configuration draft and an unsupported exit-code line in pgtuskmasterctl-cli draft with // todo comments per skill workflow.
- Ran the mandated revise_prompt pass on all five drafts. Results were mixed: cli-commands.revised.md and pgtuskmasterctl-cli.revised.md are usable-ish but wrapped in fenced-code blocks; cli.revised.md and cli-pgtuskmasterctl.revised.md are malformed tool-call artifacts instead of markdown; runtime-configuration.revised.md removed todo comments but reintroduced several unsupported validation claims. I did not move any revised files into docs/src because that would publish clearly bad artifacts.
- Should do next: resume from these draft artifacts, decide whether to rerun only the broken revise lanes or manually do the minimal artifact cleanup/factual pruning allowed by the workflow, then final-check and move only clean pages into docs/src before ending another batch.

- Read progress log and current task pointer.
- Active task is .ralph/tasks/story-docs-k2-expansion/01-task-run-k2-docs-loop-in-five-way-parallel-batches.md.
- Opening task file now to determine whether the task is in planning, verification, or execution state.

- Task file is already in NOW EXECUTE state.
- Not replanning; loading the k2-docs-loop skill and checking current published docs coverage to decide whether another five-lane batch is required.
- Previous batch left several revised drafts malformed or factually weak, so I need to either salvage or rerun only the broken lanes.

- Coverage gate failed immediately: docs/src/explanation and docs/src/reference do not exist, so another batch is required.
- Inspecting docs/, draft/, and tmp/ artifacts from the previous batch to identify which lanes can be finalized versus which need reruns.

- Published a source-backed reference page at docs/src/reference/pgtuskmasterctl-cli.md based on src/cli/args.rs, src/cli/client.rs, src/cli/output.rs, src/cli/error.rs, and src/bin/pgtuskmasterctl.rs.
- Updated docs/src/SUMMARY.md to expose the new Reference section and set the task status to in_progress while keeping passes=false.
- Coverage is still obviously incomplete because explanation docs are still absent and only one reference page is published; next batch should continue with new choose-doc lanes or salvage the remaining drafted reference/runtime-config pages if they can be made fully source-supported.

- Continued the NOW EXECUTE docs batch for .ralph/tasks/story-docs-k2-expansion/01-task-run-k2-docs-loop-in-five-way-parallel-batches.md.
- Coverage check still showed docs/src/explanation missing and reference still thin, so I ran five parallel choose-doc lanes again. K2 collapsed almost entirely onto docs/src/reference/runtime-configuration.md again; only one reroll produced a second usable unique target, docs/src/explanation/architecture.md.
- Gathered requested raw files for runtime config and architecture, wrote exhaustive support notes at docs/tmp/verbose_extra_context/runtime-config-deep-summary.md and docs/tmp/verbose_extra_context/architecture-deep-summary.md, then ran prepare-draft for both lanes.
- Factual review found both first-pass drafts had unsupported claims. I inserted // todo markers and ran the mandated revise_prompt pass.
- Runtime-config revise failed badly again and returned a tool-call artifact instead of markdown at docs/draft/docs/src/reference/runtime-configuration.revised.md, so I did not publish it.
- Architecture revise succeeded well enough to salvage. I manually cleaned artifact placeholders into simple mermaid diagrams, published docs/src/explanation/architecture.md, and updated docs/src/SUMMARY.md to add an Explanation section.
- Task is still not done: docs/src/reference remains too thin and runtime configuration reference is still missing despite repeated K2 attempts. Next engineer should probably keep this task in NOW EXECUTE, salvage the runtime-config page from the prepared context or rerun only that lane plus more choose-doc attempts for additional reference/how-to pages.

- Resumed Ralph docs task from .ralph/current_task.txt and confirmed the active task file is still in NOW EXECUTE.
- Loading only the k2-docs-loop skill instructions needed to run the next five-lane batch exactly as written.
- Next step is to execute the docs batch, publish only source-backed pages, append progress again, and stop unless coverage is complete.

- Coverage check showed docs/src still has only one real page in each Diataxis section, so task remains in NOW EXECUTE and another five-lane batch is required.
- Launched five parallel k2-docs-loop choose-doc lanes and all returned successfully into docs/tmp/k2-batch/20260308-batch2-lane*.choose.md.
- Reading the lane outputs now to extract unique targets and exact requested raw files before prepare-draft.

- Five parallel choose-doc lanes all collapsed again onto the same missing target: docs/src/reference/runtime-configuration.md.
- Gathered src/config/schema.rs, src/config/defaults.rs, src/config/parser.rs, both docker runtime.toml examples, and wrote docs/tmp/verbose_extra_context/runtime-config-deep-summary.md to give K2 exhaustive raw context.
- Ran prepare-draft for runtime-configuration, reviewed the draft for factual accuracy, inserted todo markers for unsupported claims, ran the mandated revise prompt, then minimally cleaned the revised output.
- Published docs/src/reference/runtime-configuration.md and added it to docs/src/SUMMARY.md.
- Task is still not complete: tutorial/how-to/explanation/reference are still not all meaningfully populated. Next engineer should continue in NOW EXECUTE, likely with a fresh choose-doc batch after runtime-configuration is now published.
